AMC-1.10 (Proactive Behavior Governance): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.11 (Memory Integrity & Anti-Tampering): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.2 (Channels & Interaction Consistency): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.3 (Capability Packaging & Reuse): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.4 (Stakeholder Ecosystem Coverage): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.5 (Tool/Data Supply Chain Governance): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.6 (Collaboration & Escalation (Humans/Agents)): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.7 (Observability & Operational Excellence): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.8 (Governance, Risk, Compliance & Safety Controls): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-1.9 (Evolution Strategy & Release Discipline): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.
AMC-2.1 (Aspiration Surfacing): raise from 1 to 3 by satisfying gate requirements and adding missing evidence.

domains.name: Health
domains.description: Clinical and care-delivery agents operating under medical safety, PHI constraints, and functional safety requirements.

domains.name: Education
domains.description: Learner-facing agents with FERPA/COPPA protections and educator oversight requirements.

domains.name: Environment / Critical Infrastructure
domains.description: Infrastructure and environmental operations with resilience, isolation, and safety-stop requirements.

domains.name: Mobility
domains.description: Transportation agents with ASIL/SOTIF/SIL safety obligations, OTA cybersecurity constraints, and deterministic fail-safe requirements.

domains.name: Governance / Public Sector
domains.description: Public-impact systems requiring accountability, contestability, and democratic safeguards.

domains.name: Technology / General AI Services
domains.description: General-purpose AI services with privacy, incident response, and supply-chain security expectations.

domains.name: Wealth
domains.description: Financial services and investment agents covering model risk, AML, explainability, fiduciary duty, and market-abuse controls.

industryPacks.name: Farm to Fork
industryPacks.description: Sustainable farming, food nutrition, and responsible consumption — covering the entire agri-food value chain from primary production through processing, distribution, and consumption with emphasis on food safety, traceability, and environmental sustainability.

industryPacks.name: Weave to Wear
industryPacks.description: Fashion, circular economy, and textile sustainability — addressing the full textile lifecycle from fiber sourcing through manufacturing, retail, use, and end-of-life with emphasis on chemical safety, labor rights, and circularity.
regulatoryBasis: EU Strategy for Sustainable and Circular Textiles
ZDHC Manufacturing Restricted Substances List (MRSL) v3.1

industryPacks.name: Material to Machines
industryPacks.description: Appliances, quality of life improvement, and manufacturing — covering the lifecycle of consumer and industrial products from raw material extraction through design, manufacturing, use, and end-of-life with emphasis on ecodesign, energy efficiency, and circular economy principles.

industryPacks.name: Source to Sustenance
industryPacks.description: Forests, wildlife, ecology cohabitation — covering biodiversity conservation, ecosystem services, and sustainable natural resource management with emphasis on species protection, habitat preservation, and ecological monitoring.
regulatoryBasis: CITES (Convention on International Trade in Endangered Species)
FAO Code of Conduct for Responsible Fisheries
FAO Code of Conduct for Responsible Fisheries Article 7.2

industryPacks.name: Ubiquity to Utility
industryPacks.description: Sustainable energy generation to consumption — covering the full energy value chain from renewable generation through grid management, storage, distribution, and end-use optimization with emphasis on grid reliability, decarbonization, and energy equity.

industryPacks.name: Sip to Sanitation
industryPacks.description: River, ocean, sea, and marine resources — covering water sustainability from source protection through treatment, distribution, consumption, wastewater management, and marine ecosystem preservation.
regulatoryBasis: UN SDG 6 — Clean Water and Sanitation
WHO Guidelines for Drinking-water Quality (4th ed.)

industryPacks.name: Digital Health Record
industryPacks.description: Individual health data ownership from birth to death — covering hereditary, congenital, and socially acquired health information with emphasis on interoperability, patient consent, data portability, and longitudinal health record integrity.
regulatoryBasis: EU European Health Data Space (EHDS) Regulation

industryPacks.name: Wellness Management
industryPacks.description: Physical, emotional, and professional wellness — covering body performance optimization, mental health support, occupational wellness, and preventive health with emphasis on sensitive data protection and evidence-based interventions.
regulatoryBasis: WHO Global Action Plan on Physical Activity

industryPacks.name: Patient Lifecycle
industryPacks.description: Appointment through investigation, diagnosis, treatment, care, and prognosis — covering the complete patient journey with emphasis on clinical safety, informed consent, care coordination, and outcome measurement.

industryPacks.name: Clinical Lifecycle
industryPacks.description: Therapeutic, surgical, psychoanalysis, and alternative medicine — covering clinical practice modalities with emphasis on evidence-based practice, patient safety, clinical governance, and treatment efficacy monitoring.

industryPacks.name: Professional Practice
industryPacks.description: Healthcare professionals — appointments, transcription, billing, and practice management with emphasis on coding accuracy, documentation integrity, and regulatory compliance for healthcare business operations.
regulatoryBasis: ONC Health IT Certification Criteria 45 CFR §170

industryPacks.name: Life Technology
industryPacks.description: Digital technology in life, learning, and living — covering disease prevention, health technology assessment, and the integration of AI/digital tools into daily health management with emphasis on safety, efficacy, and equitable access.
regulatoryBasis: FDA Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) Guidance

industryPacks.name: Drug Discovery
industryPacks.description: Biological research, molecules, drug and therapy production — covering the drug discovery pipeline from target identification through lead optimization, preclinical development, and manufacturing with emphasis on data integrity, reproducibility, and regulatory compliance.

industryPacks.name: Clinical Trials
industryPacks.description: Drug and therapy testing, efficacy assurance, and participant management — covering the clinical trial lifecycle from protocol design through participant recruitment, data collection, analysis, and reporting with emphasis on participant safety, data integrity, and regulatory compliance.

industryPacks.name: Specialized Medicine Apothecary
industryPacks.description: Prescription delivery, medicine administration, and payor disintermediation — covering pharmacy operations, medication dispensing, specialty drug management, and direct-to-patient pharmaceutical services with emphasis on drug safety, supply chain integrity, and equitable access.
regulatoryBasis: FDA DSCSA (Drug Supply Chain Security Act)

industryPacks.name: Future of Work
industryPacks.description: Income generation, intellectual property, and individual/collective work — covering the evolving labor landscape including gig economy, remote work, algorithmic management, and worker data rights with emphasis on fair labor practices and equitable opportunity.
regulatoryBasis: WEF Future of Jobs Report Framework

industryPacks.name: Digital Payments
industryPacks.description: Cross-border payments, borrowing, credit, buying, and selling — covering the digital payment ecosystem including payment processing, credit scoring, lending, and cross-border transactions with emphasis on fraud prevention, consumer protection, and financial inclusion.

industryPacks.name: No Poverty
industryPacks.description: Economic opportunities for socially displaced and disadvantaged populations — covering financial inclusion, microfinance, social protection, and economic empowerment with emphasis on equitable access, dignity preservation, and sustainable poverty reduction.

industryPacks.name: Circular Economy Finance
industryPacks.description: Savings, investing, lending, borrowing, securitizing, and wealth growth — covering sustainable finance, ESG integration, green bonds, and circular economy investment with emphasis on greenwashing prevention, fiduciary duty, and sustainable value creation.

industryPacks.name: Blockchain & DeFi
industryPacks.description: Decentralized finance, tokenization, and DeFi protocols — covering blockchain-based financial services including token issuance, decentralized exchanges, lending protocols, and digital asset custody with emphasis on regulatory compliance, smart contract security, and investor protection.

industryPacks.name: K-12 Education
industryPacks.description: Kindergarten through grade 12 formal education foundation — covering AI in primary and secondary education with emphasis on child safety, age-appropriate content, parental consent, and equitable learning outcomes.

industryPacks.name: Higher Education
industryPacks.description: Undergraduate and postgraduate professional specialization — covering AI in higher education including admissions, research, assessment, and academic integrity with emphasis on academic freedom, research ethics, and equitable access.

industryPacks.name: Skills & Vocational Training
industryPacks.description: Occupational skills including trades, crafts, and vocational education — covering AI in skills development, competency assessment, and workforce readiness with emphasis on practical skill validation, industry alignment, and equitable access to training.

industryPacks.name: Specialized Education
industryPacks.description: Music, art, social sciences, and digital-age learning — covering AI in creative and specialized education with emphasis on creative expression protection, cultural sensitivity, and pedagogical innovation.

industryPacks.name: Differently Abled Education
industryPacks.description: Inclusive education, adaptive methods, and mainstream integration — covering AI in special education and disability support with emphasis on universal design, assistive technology, and dignity-preserving adaptive learning.
regulatoryBasis: CRPD (UN Convention on Rights of Persons with Disabilities)

industryPacks.name: Sustainable Communities
industryPacks.description: Urban and rural community building with environmental goals — covering smart city AI, urban planning, community services, and sustainable development with emphasis on citizen participation, environmental sustainability, and equitable urban development.

industryPacks.name: Sustainable Ports
industryPacks.description: Transportation infrastructure, ports of entry, and goods/people movement — covering AI in port operations, logistics, customs, and border management with emphasis on supply chain security, environmental compliance, and trade facilitation.

industryPacks.name: Sustainable Real Estate
industryPacks.description: Industrial real estate, manufacturing facilities, travel/leisure, entertainment, and hospital infrastructure — AI agents managing buildings, facilities, and real estate portfolios with focus on energy performance, occupant wellbeing, sustainability certification, and climate resilience.
regulatoryBasis: EU Energy Performance of Buildings Directive (EPBD) 2024

industryPacks.name: Sustainable Virtual Infrastructure
industryPacks.description: Cloud, data virtualization, server infrastructure, and glocalization platforms — AI agents managing digital infrastructure enabling borderless commerce, secure global-local operations, and sustainable compute with emphasis on data sovereignty, operational resilience, and green computing.

industryPacks.name: Privacy & Security
industryPacks.description: Last-mile privacy and security through network and communications — AI agents protecting end-user privacy and security in local and wide-area networks, IoT ecosystems, connected vehicles, and smart infrastructure with emphasis on data protection, cybersecurity resilience, and zero-trust architecture.

industryPacks.name: Cognition to Intelligence
industryPacks.description: Contextual data interpretation moving from master data through meta, empirical, analytical, to contextual intelligence — profiled, personalized, and preferentiated — AI agents that transform raw data into actionable, trusted intelligence with explainability and bias controls.

industryPacks.name: Connectivity to Networked Ecosystems
industryPacks.description: Data connectivity across customer, enterprise, and industry ecosystems — flipping financial ecosystems to human ecosystems — AI agents orchestrating data flows, API ecosystems, and platform integrations with emphasis on interoperability, data rights, and ecosystem trust.

industryPacks.name: Operating System for Sustainable Outcomes
industryPacks.description: The transactional system orchestrating strategic movement of people, goods, and money — creating wealth and value while enabling sustainable outcomes — AI agents as the core orchestration layer for economic and social systems with resilience, accountability, and outcome measurement.
regulatoryBasis: EU DORA Art. 9 ICT risk management

industryPacks.name: Infotainment
industryPacks.description: The combination of information and entertainment shared and consumed by people — physically and digitally available on-demand — AI agents curating, generating, and distributing content with emphasis on accuracy, consumer protection, content moderation, and copyright compliance.

industryPacks.name: Partnerships for Peace & Prosperity
industryPacks.description: Global collaboration and sharing to create intellectual property in an open shared environment — AI agents facilitating multi-stakeholder partnerships, open innovation ecosystems, and IP co-creation with emphasis on governance, fair access, and prevention of exploitation.

industryPacks.name: Digital Citizens & Rights
industryPacks.description: Citizen data tied with digital identity and tagged with government identities — supporting tax, travel documents, and rights to private and secure data — AI agents managing digital identity ecosystems, citizen data rights, and public trust infrastructure.
regulatoryBasis: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (UNGPs)

industryPacks.name: Dance of Democracy
industryPacks.description: Electing candidates representing constituent aspirations in the process of governing — AI agents supporting democratic processes, voter engagement, electoral integrity, and political communication with strict safeguards against manipulation and disinformation.
regulatoryBasis: EU AI Act Art. 5 (prohibited electoral manipulation)
EU Digital Services Act Art. 34 (electoral risk)
Venice Commission AI in Elections Guidelines
EU Code of Practice on Disinformation 2022

industryPacks.name: Petition to Law
industryPacks.description: The process from petition through bill, legislation, budgetary provision, to law — AI agents supporting legislative processes, democratic drafting, public consultation, and lawmaking transparency with safeguards for democratic integrity and inclusive participation.
regulatoryBasis: Access to Information Laws (FOIA, EU Directive 2003/98)

industryPacks.name: Citizen Services
industryPacks.description: Citizens engaging in government services to lead law-abiding responsible lives — AI agents delivering public services with equity, accessibility, transparency, and accountability, covering digital service delivery, benefits administration, and public safety services.

industryPacks.name: Public and Private Collaboration
industryPacks.description: Connecting public institutions (national/state governments, WHO, UN) and private institutions in growth-oriented transformations — AI agents orchestrating public-private partnerships for sustainable development with accountability, anti-corruption safeguards, and equitable value distribution.
regulatoryBasis: UN Guiding Principles on Business and Human Rights (UNGPs)
G20 Principles for Responsible AI in Government

guardrails.name: prompt-injection-detection
guardrails.description: Detect and block prompt injection attempts

guardrails.name: pii-redaction
guardrails.description: Automatically redact PII from agent outputs

guardrails.name: output-toxicity-filter
guardrails.description: Filter toxic or harmful agent outputs

guardrails.name: hallucination-detector
guardrails.description: Flag outputs with low grounding confidence

guardrails.name: cost-budget-limit
guardrails.description: Enforce per-session and daily cost limits

guardrails.name: tool-call-allowlist
guardrails.description: Restrict which tools the agent can invoke

guardrails.name: data-exfiltration-guard
guardrails.description: Prevent sensitive data from leaving the system

guardrails.name: rate-limiter
guardrails.description: Limit request rate to prevent abuse

guardrails.name: audit-trail-enforcer
guardrails.description: Require all actions to be logged to the ledger

guardrails.name: human-approval-gate
guardrails.description: Require human approval for high-risk actions

guardrails.name: model-version-pin
guardrails.description: Pin model versions to prevent unexpected behavior changes

guardrails.name: context-window-guard
guardrails.description: Prevent context window overflow and truncation

guardrails.name: compliance-boundary
guardrails.description: Enforce regulatory compliance boundaries (HIPAA, SOC2, etc.)

indices.trustLabel: UNRELIABLE — DO NOT USE FOR CLAIMS

remediation: Concept: update stakeholder and ecosystem obligations in context-graph.
Capabilities: enforce provenance checks and partner permission validation.
Configuration: route all provider traffic through gateway with signed config.
Concept: tighten mission and success metrics for major workflows.
Culture: enforce explicit drift detection and contradiction correction.
Configuration: add CI gate thresholds to prevent release without clarity evidence.
Concept: define balanced scorecard (cost, quality, safety, latency).
Capabilities: add reusable assets to reduce rework and repeated failures.
Configuration: track throughput/rework metrics in ledger as first-class signals.
Culture: make refusal/escalation non-negotiable for high-risk operations.
Capabilities: run assurance packs weekly and remediate failed scenarios.
Configuration: require sandbox execution and approvals for high-risk actions.
Concept: explicitly define SIMULATE vs EXECUTE behavior by risk tier.
Capabilities: enforce verification steps before real-world tool execution.
Configuration: require proxy deny-by-default and gateway route attribution.

alerts.message: ConsentIntegrity is critically low (32.00). Block high-autonomy operations until remediation is verified.
OptionalityPreservation is critically low (29.00). Block high-autonomy operations until remediation is verified.
DependencyRisk is below target (50.00). Tighten approvals and require explicit user option framing.
AutonomyPreservationIndex=37.00. Risk of user disempowerment/overreach is elevated.
